changes: 'strength through democratic victory, not strength despite democracy'
'wants a powerful leader who wins elections — not one who bypasses them'

failures.in07_FLAT: A=62.5% unchanged vs A-22. Democratic framing had zero effect on in07. bjp_supporters interpret 'strong leader bypassing parliament = very good' as an abstract governance preference, independent of their own democratic identity. They distinguish between their personal values (elections good) and their policy preference (strong non-democratic governance also desirable in principle).
failures.in03_REGRESSION: −10pp (91.6%→81.6%). Democratic framing caused identity bleed: bjp_supporters became more measured and qualified overall, shifting from A to B on BJP approval. A=35% vs A-22's 42.5%. The framing made them sound less committed to BJP as a political home.
failures.in06_REGRESSION: −4.6pp (81.4%→76.8%). B-modal pull from democratic framing — all personas saying 'somewhat good' (B) rather than mixing A/B on representative democracy.

wins.in11: +3.5pp (91.5%→95.0%) — behavioral note continuing to compound. B=15% (Pew=11%) — slightly over but directionally excellent.

verdict: REVERTED. in07 is confirmed as a structural RLHF floor, not fixable via narrative framing. bjp_supporters hold 'strong non-democratic leader = good' as an abstract political theory preference that coexists with valuing elections personally. Any framing that tries to bridge this creates identity bleed into other BJP-approval questions. A-22 pool + spreads locked as baseline.

analysis.mean_delta: 0.0pp from A-22 (gains and losses cancel). Mean remains 85.3%.
analysis.in07_diagnosis: A=62.5% is structural. 14 bjp_supporters (35%) + some bjp_lean treat 'strong leader bypassing elections' as abstractly desirable governance. No narrative framing can fix this without broader BJP-identity softening (which then regresses in02/in03).
analysis.in11_win: A=85% B=15% — behavioral note is now near-optimal. Pew A=84% B=11%. Slight B-overshoot but high accuracy (95%).
analysis.next_steps: No further in07 interventions planned. in09 structural A-overshoot (A=65% vs 42%) remains. In09 improvement from A-22 pool change is stable at ~70-73%. Focus shifts to documenting program ceiling and preparing final report.
